title: DNS Resolution, Address Classes, Redirects, Proxies, and SSRF · Runtime Contract: Mixed Answer Policy
summary: A host passes validation before DNS then resolves or redirects to loopback, private, link-local, metadata, IPv4-mapped, zone-scoped, or rebinding targets. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go mixed answer policy increment in validated connection-time dial policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Reject, filter, or pin mixed public and forbidden address sets under explicit fail-closed policy and causal evidence.
Bind admitted addresses to DialContext or a controlled egress proxy and verify each new connection and re-resolution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Mixed Answer Policy · Theory Mixed Answer Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7141 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to block unsafe schemes, netip classes, mixed DNS, rebinding, proxy, metadata, and redirect destinations; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only.
